text: A deer steps into the clearing, ears up. It hasn't scented you yet. The drones don't come this far into the tree line.
Wild turkey flock moving through the hollow. Elias goes still. You go still. Nobody breathes.
Smoke from a chimney, too regular to be accidental. A small group of ridge folk — suspicious but willing to trade.
An old hunting cabin, untouched. Canned goods stacked neatly, like whoever left them expected to come back.
The rear axle gives out on a switchback. Takes the better part of a day to limp to flat ground.
Elias builds a fire so small it casts almost no light. You eat what you have and watch the stars. He names three constellations without being asked.
Someone drank from a stream that looked clean. It wasn't.
